verses.text: In the morning David directs his prayer to God and looks up, waiting for an answer. He begins the day at the throne of grace. To order our first thoughts toward God is to set the whole day in His light.

In the morning David directs his prayer to God and looks up, waiting for an answer. He begins the day at the throne of grace. To order our first thoughts toward God is to set the whole day in His light.

In the morning David directs his prayer to God and looks up, waiting for an answer. He begins the day at the throne of grace. To order our first thoughts toward God is to set the whole day in His light.

God takes no pleasure in wickedness; evil cannot dwell with Him, and He hates all workers of iniquity and lying. His holiness is uncompromising. The God we approach is utterly pure, and that purity should awe and cleanse us.

God takes no pleasure in wickedness; evil cannot dwell with Him, and He hates all workers of iniquity and lying. His holiness is uncompromising. The God we approach is utterly pure, and that purity should awe and cleanse us.

God takes no pleasure in wickedness; evil cannot dwell with Him, and He hates all workers of iniquity and lying. His holiness is uncompromising. The God we approach is utterly pure, and that purity should awe and cleanse us.

Yet David comes into God's house in the multitude of His mercy and asks to be led in righteousness, with a plain path before him. Mercy makes the unworthy bold to draw near. We enter not on our merit but on His abundant grace.

Yet David comes into God's house in the multitude of His mercy and asks to be led in righteousness, with a plain path before him. Mercy makes the unworthy bold to draw near. We enter not on our merit but on His abundant grace.

He describes the false, whose throat is an open grave, and asks God to judge them in their own counsels. He leaves their fate to God. The slander of the wicked is real, but vengeance belongs to the righteous Judge.

He describes the false, whose throat is an open grave, and asks God to judge them in their own counsels. He leaves their fate to God. The slander of the wicked is real, but vengeance belongs to the righteous Judge.

Let all who trust in God rejoice and shout for joy, for He blesses the righteous and surrounds him with favour as with a shield. Trust ends in gladness. God's good will encircles His people like an unbreakable defence.

Let all who trust in God rejoice and shout for joy, for He blesses the righteous and surrounds him with favour as with a shield. Trust ends in gladness. God's good will encircles His people like an unbreakable defence.
